claims.text: Bayesian optimal experimental design provides a decision-theoretic framework for selecting experiments to maximise the expected utility of data to be collected.
claims.source_quote: Bayesian optimal experimental design (BOED) provides a powerful, decision-theoretic framework for selecting experiments so as to maximise the expected utility of the data to be collected.

claims.text: The expected information gain is often high-dimensional and strongly non-convex.
claims.source_quote: The expected information gain (EIG), for example, is often high-dimensional and strongly non-convex.

claims.text: The challenge of optimising the expected information gain is particularly acute in the batch setting where multiple experiments are designed simultaneously.
claims.source_quote: This challenge is particularly acute in the batch setting, where multiple experiments are to be designed simultaneously.

claims.text: The authors introduce a new approach to batch EIG-based BOED via a probabilistic lifting of the original optimisation problem to the space of probability measures.
claims.source_quote: In this paper, we introduce a new approach to batch EIG-based BOED via a probabilistic lifting of the original optimisation problem to the space of probability measures.

claims.text: The authors propose to optimise an entropic regularisation of the expected utility over the space of design measures.
claims.source_quote: In particular, we propose to optimise an entropic regularisation of the expected utility over the space of design measures.

claims.text: Under mild conditions, the entropic regularisation objective admits a unique minimiser that can be explicitly characterised in the form of a Gibbs distribution.
claims.source_quote: Under mild conditions, we show that this objective admits a unique minimiser, which can be explicitly characterised in the form of a Gibbs distribution.

claims.text: The resulting design law can be used directly as a randomised batch-design policy or as a computational relaxation from which a deterministic batch is extracted.
claims.source_quote: The resulting design law can be used directly as a randomised batch-design policy, or as a computational relaxation from which a deterministic batch is extracted.

claims.text: The authors consider two tractable restrictions of the full batch distribution for scalable approximations when batch size is large: a mean-field family and an i.i.d. product family.
claims.source_quote: To obtain scalable approximations when the batch size is large, we then consider two tractable restrictions of the full batch distribution: a mean-field family, and an i.i.d. product family.

claims.text: For the i.i.d. objective and formally for its mean-field extension, the authors derive the corresponding Wasserstein gradient flow.
claims.source_quote: For the i.i.d. objective, and formally for its mean-field extension, we derive the corresponding Wasserstein gradient flow

claims.text: The authors characterise the long-time behaviour of the Wasserstein gradient flow for the i.i.d. objective.
claims.source_quote: we derive the corresponding Wasserstein gradient flow, characterise its long-time behaviour

claims.text: The authors obtain particle-based algorithms via space-time discretisations of the Wasserstein gradient flow.
claims.source_quote: and obtain particle-based algorithms via space-time discretisations.

claims.text: The authors introduce doubly stochastic variants that combine interacting particle updates with Monte Carlo estimators of the EIG gradient.
claims.source_quote: We also introduce doubly stochastic variants that combine interacting particle updates with Monte Carlo estimators of the EIG gradient.

claims.text: The proposed methods demonstrate the ability to explore multimodal optimisation landscapes and obtain high-utility batches in challenging examples.
claims.source_quote: demonstrating their ability to explore multimodal optimisation landscapes and obtain high-utility batches in challenging examples.
